title: Foursquare
body: Not long ago I took another step forward in connectivity and social media, since I got the **Android** HTC Tattoo I'm always online, the €12 monthly fee I pay to stay always connected to the Internet is more than worth it, since I call less and send fewer SMS messages. Tweeting, posting photos and videos directly from my mobile and especially checking email (basically looking for anything urgent and deleting what doesn't matter) are the tasks I do most often from this gadget that has stopped being merely a telephone. Another utility that I used to do with Twitter, is now easier with [**foursquare**](http://foursquare.com). [![Foursquare](http://foursquare.com/img/press/foursquare_logo_girl.png)](http://foursquare.com) It exists for iPhone, Android and BlackBerry and basically it's an application that allows us in a very simple way, and thanks to geolocation, to indicate where we are and if the place doesn't exist we can add it and it immediately updates your status on **Twitter** and/or **Facebook**. True that at first it seems like something for geeks and/or nerds, I've gotten a bit hooked since there's a classification based on points you get according to the places you've been to or added, they don't give away anything, but it's a way for people to add locations and increase the database. With a tool like this, for example, [**11870**](http://11870.com) would have expanded its database much faster. The good thing about [foursquare](http://foursquare.com) is that they have an **API**, which means their integration in other social media opens up many possibilities for the tool. Above all I see it very useful in terms of tourism and/or restaurant recommendations and venues in general. It's a first version and I'm sure that in a short time we will see how it evolves and becomes even more social. My profile: [http://foursquare.com/user/xarop](http://foursquare.com/user/xarop) **¿[How do you think these types of tools will evolve](#comments)****?**
